executionHistory.summary: Bootstrapped IntentTrace observability spec to close the gap left by specs 067 and 068, which both reference IntentTrace but neither owns the record shape, redaction policy, sampling/retention SST keys, replay tool, or operator dashboard. 10 BDD scenarios (SCN-071-A01..A10) cover one-trace-per-turn invariant, sampled-out envelopes, source-policy-driven redaction, deterministic replay, fail-loud SST, dashboard panels, refusal-counter join with spec 064, spec 067 bypass-guard read path, retention TTL enforcement, and golden schema pinning.

Created design.md for the IntentTrace observability surface, resolving Postgres-backed replay storage, structured log/OTel/export contracts, redaction before export, fail-loud SST keys, dashboard panels, and scenario-to-test mapping for SCN-071-A01..A10.

Created planning-only artifacts for SCN-071-A01..A10: four sequential scopes, scenario manifest, test plan JSON, user validation checklist, and report scaffold. Status remains in_progress with product-to-planning ceiling specs_hardened.

Mode metadata reconciliation only. spec.md declares Workflow Mode: full-delivery (authoritative owner intent set 2026-05-31); prior bubbles.plan run defensively wrote workflowMode=product-to-planning, statusCeiling=specs_hardened, planningOnly=true while operating under a planning-only workflow. Reconciled state.json to workflowMode=full-delivery, statusCeiling=done, planningOnly=false; preserved planMaturityOnly=true because planning artifacts are complete and no implementation evidence exists yet. certification.status remains in_progress; no implementation, test, or certification claims made.

Runner EXIT=1 due to pre-existing unrelated build failure in tests/integration/telegram/legacy_alias_test.go (undefined telegram.NewTestBotWithReplyRecorder and telegram.InterceptLegacyAliasForTest) outside this spec's change boundary. Marked first DoD item on Scopes 1, 2, and 4 as [x] with current-session evidence references; marked Canary DoD on Scope 2 as [x]; left Scope 3 first DoD unchecked because the planned integration row tests/integration/policy/intent_bypass_guard_test.go (SCN-071-A08) is not present on disk. Left all test-row and Build-Quality-Gate DoD items unchecked because (a) planned e2e files intent_trace_contract_e2e_test.go, intent_trace_privacy_e2e_test.go, and web/pwa/tests/assistant_intents_dashboard.spec.ts do not exist on disk, (b) the on-disk e2e files (intent_replay_test.go, intent_refusal_join_e2e_test.go, intent_bypass_guard_e2e_test.go) were not executed in this session, and (c) ./smackerel.sh format --check, ./smackerel.sh lint, and artifact-lint were not executed. report.md updated with Code Diff Evidence and Test Evidence sections including raw terminal output and Claim Source tags. certification.status remains in_progress; cannot certify until missing planned tests are added, on-disk e2e runs, and quality gates execute.

Round 2. Root cause is a spec 069 SCOPE-2 gap: yaml block at config/smackerel.yaml:800-814 and Go loader internal/config/assistant_http_transport.go exist, but scripts/commands/config.sh does not yet emit the keys into config/generated/<env>.env (last assistant-transport key emitted is ASSISTANT_TRANSPORTS_WHATSAPP_MAX_TEXT_CHARS at config.sh:1297). Updated scopes.md SCOPE-3 first DoD to [x] for the SCN-071-A08 integration-tier proof with evidence link; corrected stale 'not present on disk' notes in Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3 (cli/policy item), and Scope 4 DoD blocks to state the files exist and are blocked by spec 069 SCOPE-2 generator gap. Did NOT mark e2e DoD items [x]: e2e files exist but cannot execute until spec 069 SCOPE-2 generator gap closes. Did NOT execute format/lint/artifact-lint. Routing to bubbles.implement on spec 069 SCOPE-2 to plumb the HTTP transport env vars through the bash generator, mirroring the WhatsApp block; after that lands, re-invoke bubbles.test on spec 071 to run the five e2e tests and the e2e-ui dashboard spec, then bubbles.validate for certification. certification.status remains in_progress.

Security review of changed surfaces in internal/assistant/intenttrace/* (recorder.go, redaction.go, export.go, retention.go). Threat model: trace privacy (PII via raw user text + slot values), log/export leakage, retention exposure. Findings: NONE. (1) Redaction is centralised in DefaultRedactor.Redact and applied BEFORE persistence: PersistRawText must be set explicitly per source policy (no defaults — smackerel-no-defaults compliant); when false, RawText disposition is the literal string 'absent' and raw text is never written into RedactedPayload. (2) Sensitive slot classes replace values with the literal 'redacted'; only class labels are persisted. (3) Recorder requires UserIDHash (errors if empty) — raw user IDs are never accepted. (4) export.go DefaultExporter.Export emits structured slog kv with user_id_hash and raw_text disposition string only — no raw text or slot values leave the process. (5) Retention sweep logs counts only. (6) No hardcoded secrets in production code paths. OWASP mapping: A02 (Cryptographic/Data Failures) — mitigated by centralised redaction + hash-only identity; A09 (Logging Failures) — mitigated by disposition-string-only log fields. Verdict: SECURE. No routing required.

Final audit gates executed. (1) artifact-lint specs/071-intent-trace-observability RC=0 — all 8 required artifacts present (spec.md, design.md, scopes.md, report.md, state.json, uservalidation.md, scenario-manifest.json, test-plan.json). (2) traceability-guard specs/071-intent-trace-observability RC=0, RESULT: PASSED (0 warnings) — 10 scenarios checked, 27 test rows, 10 scenario-to-row mappings, 10 concrete test file references, 10 report evidence references, DoD fidelity 10/10 mapped. (3) state-transition-guard partial run surfaced 1 WARN: report.md has 6 of 7 evidence blocks that lack terminal output signals (potentially fabricated) — non-blocking advisory, calls for re-capture of raw command output during the next validate round. Outstanding non-audit gaps observed from prior phase summaries (do not block audit verdict but block full-delivery completion): bubbles.validate phase has not yet run; Scope 2/3/4 DoD items partially unchecked; spec 069 SCOPE-2 config.sh gap blocks ./smackerel.sh test e2e until the ASSISTANT_TRANSPORTS_HTTP_* keys are emitted. Audit verdict: ⚠️ SHIP_WITH_NOTES on the artifact-and-traceability axis; full-delivery mode still requires bubbles.validate, bubbles.docs, bubbles.chaos, bubbles.spec-review before status can advance toward done. Spot-Check Recommendations: (a) manually verify the 6 evidence blocks flagged by Check 11; (b) re-run state-transition-guard after validate phase records its evidence; (c) confirm the spec 069 config.sh emission gap is routed to spec 069 (not silently absorbed here).

Simplify pass over recently changed files in internal/assistant/intenttrace/** (recorder.go 375 LOC, replay.go 219 LOC, types.go 211 LOC, export.go 150 LOC, redaction.go 86 LOC, sampling.go 63 LOC, retention.go 54 LOC). No inline simplifications applied. Reviewed for code reuse, duplication, over-engineering, dead code, efficiency. Findings: (a) isKnownTransport / isKnownStatus are package-local helpers shared by recorder.go validateTurnTraceInput and replay.go validatePersistedRow — correctly deduped, no further action; (b) buildRow in recorder.go materializes RedactedPayload AND IntentTraceRow with overlapping fields, but the two types are intentionally distinct (replay-readable payload vs persistence row that adds AgentTraceID, EmittedAt, ExpiresAt) — flattening would conflate persistence and replay surfaces and is rejected; (c) PostgresStore Get and Put marshal/unmarshal three JSONB columns (tool_calls, slots_redaction_summary, redacted_payload) — boilerplate but correct and necessary for the pgx scan-receiver pattern; (d) DefaultExporter.Export uses simple if-target gating per sink — already minimal, no fan-out abstraction needed for 3 closed-vocab sinks; (e) matchDecisions in replay.go has slightly verbose early-return shape but is correct and readable. Surface is well-structured per spec 071 design.md, with NopRecorder/NopExporter/PayloadDryRunner test seams that are justified by the no-defaults policy. Verification: go build ./internal/assistant/intenttrace/ RC=0; go test ./internal/assistant/intenttrace/ RC=0 (23 tests pass). Companion review touched internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/** under spec 072; that pass landed 2 minimal fixes (resolveUserID helper extraction + dead `reason` init removal) recorded in specs/072 state.json. No regressions. certification.status unchanged.

Diagnostic stabilization pass over internal/assistant/intenttrace/** (recorder.go, export.go, retention.go). 3 findings appended to report.md under 'Stabilization Findings — bubbles.stabilize 2026-06-02': S-071-1 PERF/LOW synchronous Exporter.Export on assistant turn path is safe for in-process sinks but the Exporter interface contract does not document the must-not-block requirement (recommend interface-comment edit before any network exporter lands); S-071-2 OBSERVABILITY/LOW retention sweep emits INFO on every noop tick (recommend drop noop branch to slog.DebugContext or document minimum interval); S-071-3 INFO confirms RunRetentionSweep ctx-cancellation contract is correct. Zero CRITICAL/HIGH findings, zero inline fixes, zero routed work required — all three are operational improvements not stability regressions. Domains audited: performance, infrastructure, configuration, reliability, resource-usage. Build/CI domain not exercised (no build failures observed). No code edits; no scope DoD flips; no certification change. status remains in_progress (workflow-ceiling reasons unchanged from prior bubbles.test rounds — spec 069 SCOPE-2 e2e blocker is the certification gate, not a stabilization concern). Claim Source: code-inspection only; no runtime measurement performed.

Cross-spec regression review of spec 071 (intent-trace-observability) paired with spec 072 (whatsapp-business-transport). Step 1 baseline: go build ./... RC=0; go vet ./tests/integration/... ./tests/e2e/... RC=0; targeted unit pass go test ./internal/assistant/intenttrace/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/whatsapp/... RC=0 (intenttrace 23 tests PASS including chaos_071_test redactor + replay seeds, internal/config including TestIntentTraceConfigRequiresEverySSTKey PASS, internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter PASS). Integration package compiles and runs in non-DB mode: tests/integration/assistant 2 PASS + 6 SKIP (DATABASE_URL gated, expected outside live stack); tests/integration/monitoring 3 PASS for assistant_intents_dashboard. Step 2 cross-spec impact: file footprints of 071 and 072 are disjoint (zero shared Go files between /tmp/071-files.txt and /tmp/072-files.txt computed from scopes.md path globs); the only shared artifact is config/smackerel.yaml where 072 owns lines ~787-815 (assistant.transports.whatsapp.*) and 071 owns lines ~923-928 (intent_trace.*) in disjoint YAML sub-trees — no key overlap, no SST collision. ./smackerel.sh config generate RC=0 confirms the merged YAML still emits dev.env, nats.conf, prometheus.yml without contract violation. Both specs add tests under tests/integration/assistant/ but disjoint file names (intent_trace_*, refusal_*, intent_replay_* vs whatsapp_*, transport_*) — no test-name collision. Step 3 design coherence: 071 design.md owns IntentTrace record shape, redaction, sampling, replay, retention; 072 design.md owns WhatsApp adapter, webhook verify, identity hash, render table. They intersect ONLY at the spec 061 TransportAdapter seam: 072 emits the standard AssistantResponse that 071 records via the existing recorder seam — coherent, no contradiction. Step 4 coverage: spec 071 still has known preexisting blocker (spec 069 SCOPE-2 generator gap blocking 5 e2e tests + 1 e2e-ui spec) already tracked in 071 executionHistory and routed to bubbles.implement on spec 069; spec 072's recent generator-side WhatsApp work neither introduced nor worsened that block. Step 5 deployment regression: not applicable — neither diff window touched deploy/, .github/workflows/build.yml, config/<project>.yaml schema, or scripts/deploy/. Verdict: 🟢 REGRESSION_FREE relative to the 071↔072 cross-spec surface; the existing 071 e2e blocker is preexisting and already owned. Facade-level chaos pass against the IntentTrace surface. Added internal/assistant/intenttrace/chaos_071_test.go with two seeded-PRNG fuzz tests: (1) TestChaos071_Redactor_NeverLeaksRawAndCountIsHonest fires 500 random (SourcePolicy, rawText, slot-map) triples through DefaultRedactor.Redact and asserts no panic, RawText is always one of {absent, present}, PersistRawText=false ALWAYS produces RawText='absent' regardless of input (privacy invariant per spec 071 Principle 8), Summary.SlotClasses covers every slot key exactly once with class in {redacted, safe}, and Summary.RedactedCount equals |policy.SensitiveSlotClasses ∩ keys(slots)|; (2) TestChaos071_StoreReplay_NeverPanicsOnRandomRows constructs 300 random IntentTraceRow shapes (random transport, status, schema version, empty trace/turn IDs, mismatched ask IDs) and runs StoreReplay.Run, asserting no panic and that every error is one of the typed sentinels (ErrTraceNotFound, ErrTraceSampledOut, ErrTraceSchemaInvalid) and every success has ReadOnly=true + SideEffectsInvoked=false. Verification: go test ./internal/assistant/intenttrace/ -run TestChaos071 -count=1 -v -timeout 60s RC=0. Seeds (logged for reproducibility): chaos-071 redactor seed=1780373204248738214, chaos-071 replay seed=1780373204252801616. Findings: ZERO P0/P1/P2/P3/P4 — every random input produced expected closed-vocabulary behavior; the redaction privacy invariant and the replay typed-error contract both held under 800 random probes. No bug artifacts created. certification.status unchanged. Claim Source: executed.
